title: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of any local ordinances/bylaws (under RSA 31:39) and other policies regulating conflicts of interest, financial disclosures, reporting requirements and incompatibility standards for municipial officers and employees.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Fulfilment of Request Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Audrey and Jonathan,
Attached please find the fulfillment of your records request made on May 15, 2026, from the Town of Salem, NH.
If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Ciara Salazar

Assistant to the Town Managers Office / Communications Coordinator

Town of Salem

33 Geremonty Drive

Salem, NH 03079

csalazar@salemnh.gov<mailto:csalazar@salemnh.gov>

(603) 685-6421



Before printing think green; read on the screen.



This message contains information intended solely for the individual named. Please notify the sender immediately by e-mail if you have received this e-mail by mistake and delete this e-mail from your system.



E-mail sent and received on the Town's system is subject to potential disclosure under N.H. R.S.A. 91-A and in litigation unless exempt by law.  Users of the e-mail system should be aware that e-mail is not a private or confidential means of communication

title: Christman American Petroleum Institute 1

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Christman American Petroleum Institute 1
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all written or electronic communications, including text messages, Signal messages, emails and attachments, between Senior Advisor Jordan Christman and any staff member, lobbyist, employee, director or agent of the American Petroleum Institute.

This request seeks records produced since April 1, 2025. 

I am a reporter for Public Domain and seek a fee waiver. If that waiver is denied i am willing to pay $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

title: The United States Department of Transportation, Washington DC

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: The United States Department of Transportation, Washington DC
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any investigation of double dipping, forgery, or fraud of the McLennan County Sheriff's department. All memos, emails, letters, reports, or any communications concerning any allegations of double dipping by McLennan County Sheriff's officers, Nixon, Eubank, Eubank, Wilcock, Prevynovak, and others beginning in 2021 until the present. This could be out of your Ft.Worth office and REginald Lee.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bernadette Feazell

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #OIG-2022-0079
communications.communication:
I, with informants inside and outside of the McLennan County Sheriff's department, under the Public Information Act, got copies of the Sheriff's deputies time sheets, and compared these sheets to the sheets obtained through the Texas Department of Transportation,  we also sent them to the United States Dept. of Transportation and I received a call from Reginald Lee of the Ft. Worth Office. My group and I put ourselves at risk and proved that indeed the McLennan County Sheriff deputies, about twelve of them, were double dipping, it was obvious in the comparison of the documents.

Both Reginald Lee and Holly  Zakarias talked to me, thanked us for our work, and since I obtained these records through MUCKROCK, they're online and publc forever.

Informants called Reginald Lee and we all sat around and waited. We waited and waited. We're still waiting.

Something happened and this entire thing got closed down and forgotten. WHAM. None of us can even get in touch with Reginald Lee or Holly Zakarias. 

We are in danger and this is a travesty, please do something.
B

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #OIG-2022-0079
communications.communication: I have documents showing I talked with Reginald Lee, this is a terrible thing to do to us, the ones who put this together.  TXDot seems to have dropped everything, nothing shows up, just like with you. THIS is a terrible thing to do to people who still care about corruption, especially in Texas where the crooks run free over the rest of us and you just let them do it. Unreal. This is so shameful. Guess nothing matters at all anymore. Thanks for tipping me over giving up or not. I give up, the crooks and those who let this go on, you, have won.

On Sept. 18, 2023:
Subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #OIG-2022-0079
I have documents showing I talked with Reginald Lee, this is a terrible thing to do to us, the ones who put this together. TXDot seems to have dropped everything, nothing shows up, just like with you. THIS is a terrible thing to do to people who still care about corruption, especially in Texas where the crooks run free over the rest of us and you just let them do it. Unreal. This is so shameful. Guess nothing matters at all anymore. Thanks for tipping me over giving up or not. I give up, the crooks and those who let this go on, you, have won.
---

On Feb. 17, 2023:
Subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #OIG-2022-0079
I, with informants inside and outside of the McLennan County Sheriff's department, under the Public Information Act, got copies of the Sheriff's deputies time sheets, and compared these sheets to the sheets obtained through the Texas Department of Transportation, we also sent them to the United States Dept. of Transportation and I received a call from Reginald Lee of the Ft. Worth Office. My group and I put ourselves at risk and proved that indeed the McLennan County Sheriff deputies, about twelve of them, were double dipping, it was obvious in the comparison of the documents.

Both Reginald Lee and Holly Zakarias talked to me, thanked us for our work, and since I obtained these records through MUCKROCK, they're online and publc forever.

Informants called Reginald Lee and we all sat around and waited. We waited and waited. We're still waiting.

Something happened and this entire thing got closed down and forgotten. WHAM. None of us can even get in touch with Reginald Lee or Holly Zakarias.

We are in danger and this is a travesty, please do something.
B

---

title: ShotSpotter Contract (Miami Police Department)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: ShotSpotter Contract (Miami Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the current contract between this department, the city, and ShotSpotter, Inc. This request includes the full ShotSpotter Services Agreement, Standard Terms and Conditions, terms of service (including square miles covered, total cost, and contract beginning and end dates), any reference service level agreements, contract amendments, cure notices, non-disclosure agreements, or other revisions to agreements.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Katie Ryan

communications.subject: Fw: Florida Sunshine Law Request #W032965-072522
communications.communication:
Greetings Katie.
The ShotSpotter agreement you requested on July 25, 2022, was emailed to you through the GovQA platform on November 17, 2022.

If you require any additional information, please direct future correspondence to the PIO email address rather than the SMU email account.

It is my understanding that the MuckRock platform sends automated follow-up emails requesting updates on public records requests until the requester manually closes or completes the request. If that is the case, please disable the automatic update function for this request to avoid unnecessary back-and-forth communications.

In the event you did not receive the “ShotSpotter Respond Services Agreement” (formerly known as “ShotSpotter Flex”), along with the associated terms of service — including the square mileage covered, the total cost incurred since Miami began using ShotSpotter, and the contract’s “go-live” date — as part of the original public records request you referenced, I will reopen the request and include this notation.



Thank you.


Orlando Rodriguez
Public Relations Agent
Public Information Office
City of Miami Police Department
400 N.W. 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33128
[cid:c68558aa-b9ef-4e75-8b26-6cb7ee296094]

title: 2026 Brady List (Adams County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Adams County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse
Attached is the PID you requested. This will complete your PRA request.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Adams County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your quick response. As mentioned in my original request, would you please also provide the accompanying impeachment materials for each officer on the list?

Thank you,
Rose

title: Performance Evaluation

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation(s) for the current city/town manager and administrator. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Rochester Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Rochester Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of any local ordinances/bylaws (under RSA 31:39) and other policies regulating conflicts of interest, financial disclosures, reporting requirements and incompatibility standards for municipial officers and employees.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Rochester Clerk)
communications.communication:
The portion of our Charter related to Conflicts of Interest can be found online: https://ecode360.com/32212208
Incompatibility standards are also available within our online ordinances: https://ecode360.com/32216407 and https://ecode360.com/32216408

title: Community ALPR Audit (Bloomington Police Department)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit (Bloomington Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Justification Records
All records used to justify adoption of Flock Safety cameras, including but not limited to: staff reports, city council or county board memoranda, crime analysis reports, presentations, vendor proposals, and any other documents that described the purpose, anticipated benefit, or stated rationale for purchasing or leasing the system.

2. Effectiveness and Use Statistics
All statistical reports, internal summaries, use-justification documents, crime-reduction assessments, or after-action reviews evaluating the effectiveness of Flock Safety cameras since installation. This includes any records prepared in connection with contract renewals.

3. Cost and Financial Records
All financial records related to Flock Safety cameras, including invoices, purchase orders, annual subscription payments, installation costs, and maintenance costs from the date of first installation through the present. Include any records showing federal, state, grant, or civil asset forfeiture funding used to offset costs.

4. Camera Inventory and Placement
The current inventory or deployment list of Flock Safety cameras operated by or accessible to this agency, including number of units and general location descriptions. Also include any written policies, criteria, risk assessments, or internal documents used to determine camera placement locations.

5. Contracts and Legal Agreements
Copies of all active and recently expired contracts, amendments, renewals, data-sharing agreements, memoranda of understanding, and scope-of-work documents between this agency and Flock Safety.

6. Data Access and Network Sharing
(a) The Flock "Organization Audit" report showing all searches made within this agency's Flock system from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(b) The Flock "Network Audit" report showing all searches of this agency's Flock data by any outside agency from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(c) A list of all agencies currently authorized to access this agency's Flock data, including any federal agencies.
Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet).

7. Security Incidents and Unauthorized Access
All records documenting any security incidents, unauthorized access, data breaches, hacking events, or unauthorized sharing of data collected by Flock Safety cameras, including any correspondence with or notifications from Flock Safety related to such incidents.

8. Secretary of State Audit
All records, correspondence, or internal communications related to the Illinois Secretary of State's 2025 audit of ALPR data-sharing practices, including any communications with the Secretary of State's office or Flock Safety regarding compliance.

9. Marketing and Sales Materials
All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials shared with this agency by Flock Safety or any representative thereof prior to or in connection with the purchase or renewal of the Flock Safety system.

10. Communications with Flock Safety
All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

has been identified as needing to be narrowed. Fulfilling the request as currently stated has been determined to be unduly burdensome pursuant to Section 3(g) of the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140). Specifically, but without limitation, providing the records would be unduly burdensome to the City for the following reasons: 
  
 A narrow is needed on your request as part of request asking for emails is burdensome. Looking only for emails with @flocksafety.com results in 71,970 matches.  PD_AdditionalInfoTo Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 1. Justification Records All records used to justify adoption of Flock Safety cameras, including but not limited to: staff reports, city council or county board memoranda, crime analysis reports, presentations, vendor proposals, and any other documents that described the purpose, anticipated benefit, or stated rationale for purchasing or leasing the system. 2. Effectiveness and Use Statistics All statistical reports, internal summaries, use-justification documents, crime-reduction assessments, or after-action reviews evaluating the effectiveness of Flock Safety cameras since installation. This includes any records prepared in connection with contract renewals. 3. Cost and Financial Records All financial records related to Flock Safety cameras, including invoices, purchase orders, annual subscription payments, installation costs, and maintenance costs from the date of first installation through the present. Include any records showing federal, state, grant, or civil asset forfeiture funding used to offset costs. 4. Camera Inventory and Placement The current inventory or deployment list of Flock Safety cameras operated by or accessible to this agency, including number of units and general location descriptions. Also include any written policies, criteria, risk assessments, or internal documents used to determine camera placement locations. 5. Contracts and Legal Agreements Copies of all active and recently expired contracts, amendments, renewals, data-sharing agreements, memoranda of understanding, and scope-of-work documents between this agency and Flock Safety. 6. Data Access and Network Sharing (a) The Flock "Organization Audit" report showing all searches made within this agency's Flock system from the date of installation through the date of this request. (b) The Flock "Network Audit" report showing all searches of this agency's Flock data by any outside agency from the date of installation through the date of this request. (c) A list of all agencies currently authorized to access this agency's Flock data, including any federal agencies. Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet). 7. Security Incidents and Unauthorized Access All records documenting any security incidents, unauthorized access, data breaches, hacking events, or unauthorized sharing of data collected by Flock Safety cameras, including any correspondence with or notifications from Flock Safety related to such incidents. 8. Secretary of State Audit All records, correspondence, or internal communications related to the Illinois Secretary of State's 2025 audit of ALPR data-sharing practices, including any communications with the Secretary of State's office or Flock Safety regarding compliance. 9. Marketing and Sales Materials All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials shared with this agency by Flock Safety or any representative thereof prior to or in connection with the purchase or renewal of the Flock Safety system. 10. Communications with Flock Safety All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. title: Public Records Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
On April 30, 2026, you submitted a request for records to the Wellesley Police Department (the Department) under the Public Records Law for the following information:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.
The responsive record(s) are included with this response. The audit reports have been narrowly redacted pursuant to Exemption (a), Exemption (c) and Exemption (f) to the Public Records Law.


Exemption (a) allows the withholding of materials or data when there is a law that either exempts the information from being a public record or the law prohibits the information from being released to the public.  If the law exempts the information the government agency is not compelled to release it.



Under Captured G.L. c. 66A, license plate data from automated scanners is considered personal data or sensitive intelligence data retained only for specific investigations and therefore has been redacted from the audit reports.



Exemption (c) allows the withholding of “materials or data relating to a specifically named individual, the disclosure of which may constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.” G.L. c. 4, §7(26)(c).  Analysis under Exemption (c) requires balancing the public’s right to know against the relevant privacy interests at stake. Torres v. Att’y Gen., 391, Mass. 1, 9 (1984). Determinations must be made on a case-by-case basis. The factors to consider when assessing the weight of the privacy interest at stake are: (1) whether disclosure would result in personal embarrassment to an individual of normal sensibilities; (2) whether the materials sought contain intimate details of a highly personal nature; and (3) whether the same information is available from other sources. See People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals (PETA) v. Dep’t of Agric. Res., 477 Mass. 280, 292 (2017).


The audit reports contain license plate numbers which could result in an unwarranted invasion of privacy to an individual if released.  The information also would not shed light on whether government officials are carrying out their duties in a law-abiding and efficient manner. Therefore, public interest in disclosing this information does not outweigh these individual’s privacy interests.

Exemption (f) protects “investigatory materials necessarily compiled out of the public view by law enforcement or other investigatory officials the disclosure of which materials would probably so prejudice the possibility of effective law enforcement that such disclosure would not be in the public interest.” G. L. c. 4, § 7(26)(f). The exemption allows investigative officials to withhold materials that could compromise investigative efforts if disclosed. A Guide to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, at 21. Redactions under this exemption may include those that preserve the anonymity of voluntary witnesses and protect confidential investigative techniques. Id.

The audit reports contain license plate numbers that are part of active and ongoing investigations.  Upon review, the Police Department believes that, if released, this information could hinder future investigative efforts by the Department. Therefore, it is in the public interest to withhold this information. As the Department will continue to use these techniques for investigations going forward, this analysis does not change just because the investigation is suspended.


You have a right to appeal this determination to the Supervisor of Records under M.G.L. c.66, Section 10A(a), and the right to seek judicial review of an unfavorable decision by commencing a civil action in the superior court under subsection (c) of Section 10A.

title: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / Bob Carter Communications

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / Bob Carter Communications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All communications — including text messages, emails, direct messages, iMessages, Signal messages, WhatsApp messages, or any other written or electronic communications — between Travis Johnson, Chief of Staff for the Office of the Attorney General, and Bob Carter (Vice President, Flock Safety), including communications to or from bob.carter@flocksafety.com or any other known contact information for Mr. Carter, on any government-issued device or government account, from January 1, 2025 through the present.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by email attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

If you contend that any responsive records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation identifying the specific statutory basis for withholding each record individually.

Please provide records in their original electronic format, including all available metadata.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / Bob Carter Communications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Georgia Open Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on May 6, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / Bob Carter Communications
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Georgia Department of Law, Office of the Attorney General, regarding your Open Records request.

We have identified relevant communications with Bob Carter.

Please note that we charge for the time spent processing your request.  The fee for fulfilling your request is calculated based on the time and expertise of the administrative staff and attorneys involved.

This includes searching, retrieving, and reviewing all relevant documents, redacting information exempted by the Georgia Open Records Act or other applicable laws, and combining documents into a single file for delivery.
Please be aware that documents may contain information that is exempt from disclosure under the Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-72, or other applicable laws.
We anticipate that the time required to review and compile responsive records will be in the range of 5 business days.  We also estimate that the cost to respond to your request will be around $43.45.  This includes 1.35 hours of paralegal time at $20 per hour and .17 hours of attorney management time at $61.50 per hour.  Please note that this is an estimate, and the final timeline and fee may vary.  We reserve the right to revise these estimates as our review continues.

If you wish to proceed, please confirm in writing that you will be responsible for payment of the costs of response.  Thank you.


Cordially,

Open Records
Office of the Attorney General
Georgia Department of Law
40 Capitol Square SW
Atlanta, Georgia 30334
(404) 458-3600

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia AG — Travis Johnson / Bob Carter Communications
communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Before authorizing payment, I would note that O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(c) provides that the first 15 minutes of search, retrieval, and review time are provided at no cost. Your estimate does not appear to reflect this deduction. Please confirm the adjusted cost estimate before I authorize payment.

Sincerely,

title: Public Records Request: Hess

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Hess
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Metadata for email communications where Council Member Bo Hess is either the sender, recipient, CC'ed, or BCC'ed, sent between the dates of 1/1/2025 and 5/13/2026 and containing at least one of the following keywords: "LPRs", "Fusus", "Flock", "RTIC", "plate readers", "Sunshine" or "Kim".

The specific metadata data points I'm interested in are: to address(es), from address, cc address(es), bcc address(es), sent date/time, received date/time, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, and only the specified email metadata. Please do not include duplicate emails. Please provide me with the records in an Excel format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

e

title: Public Information Request: PenLink

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Public Information Request: PenLink
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Pen-Link LTD that were active any time between 1/1/2023 and 4/30/2026.
- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Pen-Link LTD. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.
- A copy of all training materials, including but not limited to any handouts, workbooks, assessments, presentations, slides, videos, audio files, or other materials, either developed in-house or provided to the agency pertaining to the agency's use of Pen-Link LTD products.
- A copy of all agency manuals either developed in-house or provided to the agency pertaining to the agency's use of Pen-Link LTD products.
- A copy of all agency policies and procedures either developed in-house or provided to the agency pertaining to the agency's use of Pen-Link LTD products.
- A roster of all agency employees who either have a user account for Pen-Link LTD products or have internal agency permissions to use Pen-Link LTD products as of 4/30/2026.
- A roster of all agency employees who have completed agency training for the use of Pen-Link LTD products as of 4/30/2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis

communications.subject: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Technical Specifications: Any Interface Control Document (ICD) or technical implementation guide that defines the data structure and data elements for the automated export of Records Management System (RMS) or Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) data to the "CISC Data Warehouse" or the LexisNexis "PSDEX private container".
2) Data Mapping: Any "Data Translation" guides, field-mapping tables, or schemas used to configure the transfer of local agency data into the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Virtual Crime Center (VCC) platforms.
3) Field Lists: Any record or "Schedule A" line item defining the "Database Interface" capabilities, specifically identifying which categories of information (e.g., Victim PII, Witness Contact Data, Real-Time Jail Bookings) are included in the nightly (or regular) automated feed.

Exclusion Note: We are not seeking URLs, passwords, network IP addresses, encryption keys, or sensitive security information, or contents of any data fields. We are seeking only the field name of the data elements (e.g., "Name," "DOB," "SSN," "Narrative") that have been approved for mass transfer to LexisNexis/PSDEX.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Re: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
Dear Alan J. Segal,

Thank you for your Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) request. After reviewing the items you listed, I want to clarify the role of the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) in relation to the systems and data connections you referenced.

CISC does not create, host, or manage any technical data interfaces, data feeds, or system‑to‑system connections for RMS or CAD exports. All data connections, data translation processes, interface specifications, and technical documentation associated with the Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX), the Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC), or the “CISC Data Warehouse” environment are developed, maintained, and administered solely by our contracted vendor, LexisNexis.

Because of this structure:

  *   CISC does not possess Interface Control Documents (ICDs), data‑mapping guides, field‑translation tables, or technical specifications for RMS/CAD exports.
  *   CISC does not configure, review, or approve the specific data elements that any individual agency elects to share with LexisNexis.
  *   Any “Schedule A,” field list, or definition of what data elements an agency provides is an agreement directly between that agency and LexisNexis, not CISC.
  *   CISC does not receive, store, or maintain copies of those documents and therefore has no responsive records to provide under CORA.

If you are seeking the technical specifications or field‑level documentation for the PSDEX/VCC data interfaces, you may wish to request those materials directly from LexisNexis or from the individual agencies that have executed data‑sharing agreements with them.

Sincerely,


Robert Nanney

Executive Director

720-397-9471

[Logo of the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium.]

https://cisc.colorado.gov<https://cisc.colorado.gov/>

communications.subject: RE: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
Hi Mr. Nanney, 

Thank you for the detailed response, I appreciate the information. 

A few quick clarifications:
1) You mentioned asking for the details from LexisNexis directly - that seems like the most efficient way to answer my questions and wouldn't require member agencies to deal with CORA requests. Are you able to provide contact info for an account manager at LexisNexis that may be able to provide the information I requested?
2) I see 120 CISC member agencies listed on the website. You mentioned reaching out to them directly if needed. Do they all have the data sharing agreements or just a subset? If it's just a subset, do you mind letting me know which ones? I don't want to waste anyone's time with a request for documents they don't have. 
3) As I was learning more about your agency I noticed the most recent meeting minutes available were from Sept. 2024. Is there any way to view more recent meeting minutes? 

Thank you again for the help! Have a great week. 

Best,
Alan

communications.subject: Re: Colorado Open Records Act Request: Colorado Information Sharing Consortium (CISC) - Data Transfer to LexisNexis
communications.communication:
Dear Alan J. Segal,



Thank you for your follow-up.



1. LexisNexis / vendor contact

CISC does not maintain a designated LexisNexis contact for purposes of responding to public records requests. As noted previously, the technical materials you are seeking (e.g., interface specifications, field mappings, or data schemas) are created and maintained by LexisNexis or by individual member agencies in connection with their implementations. You may wish to direct those requests to the individual agencies. We do not maintain a list of each agency’s CORA contact person.



2. Member agency participation and agreements

CISC has a master agreement with LexisNexis. Member agencies that elect to participate do so by executing a joinder (referred to as an addendum and joinder). By executing that document, a member agency becomes a party to the master agreement as a “Customer.” We are providing a copy of the master agreement with LexisNexis, along with the standard joinder form, for your reference.



3. “Schedule A” (what it is and what it is not)

We are also providing a redacted copy of the applicable Schedule A to the agreement. This was redacted by LexisNexis to remove their confidential commercial information as permitted under CORA.



To clarify terminology:

  *   Schedule A in this agreement is a service and pricing schedule that sets forth the LexisNexis products, features, and subscription terms provided under the contract.
  *
It contains items such as subscription fees, product features, and related terms and conditions.
  *
It does not identify or define the specific data elements that any CISC member agency contributes to or shares through the system.

Under the agreement structure, Schedule A governs the services being provided, while decisions regarding what data is contributed or shared are made by individual member agencies.

4. Data contributions and control
By way of clarification:

  *   Each participating agency contributes its own law enforcement data to the system (referred to as “Customer Data Contributions”).
  *
That data is stored in the CISC Regional Data Warehouse (a PSDEX private container maintained by LexisNexis).
  *
Each agency is responsible for the content of its data and retains ownership of that data.

Importantly, data-sharing is controlled at the agency level:

  *   By default, data contributed by a member agency is shared within the CISC consortium.
  *
Sharing beyond the consortium (e.g., with other law enforcement agencies outside CISC or other third parties) occurs only if and to the extent the contributing agency affirmatively authorizes that sharing.
  *
Agencies may tailor, limit, or revoke those permissions.

CISC does not determine what data an agency contributes, does not control what data elements are included in any feed, and does not maintain agency-specific field lists, mappings, or similar technical documentation.

5. Board meeting minutes
CISC maintains board meeting minutes and will provide copies of all minutes in our possession responsive to your request. For convenience, some minutes are also posted on the CISC website, although that posting may not always reflect the most current set.

Please let me know if you have any additional questions.

Sincerely,


Robert Nanney

Executive Director

720-397-9471

[Logo of the Colorado Information Sharing Consortium.]

https://cisc.colorado.gov<https://cisc.colorado.gov/>

title: Capitol Display Installation Records (“Thank You Governor Walz”)

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Capitol Display Installation Records (“Thank You Governor Walz”)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records, documents, and communications generated, received, or maintained by your agency related to the display officially titled "Thank You Gov. Walz" as listed on its permit, which was located in the Minnesota State Capitol from April 2, 2025, through April 15, 2025. This includes records referring to the display by its official title ('Thank You Gov. Walz') or by descriptive references, including any communications containing language about a 'Democratic coalition of Satan worshippers.'

I request the following specific categories of records:

1. All communications (including emails, letters, text messages, or memos) regarding scheduling, setup, or removal of this display
2. Any records containing contact information for the individual(s) or entity that requested, applied for, or coordinated the display, or their representatives (including email addresses, phone numbers, or mailing addresses).
3. Any work orders, setup requests, or facilities coordination documents
4. Any incident reports, complaints, or internal discussions related to the display
5. Any records documenting any review of the display's adherence to applicable laws, regulations, or agency policies, including internal communications, legal opinions, and compliance assessments, excluding privileged materials.

This request excludes automated news alerts, media monitoring summaries, or bulk-distributed news digests unless they contain internal commentary.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Rachel Cooper

title: GCUPD Policies

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: GCUPD Policies
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request a copy of the policy manual and/or general operating procedures of the police department for Grand Canyon University.

A.R.S 15-1897(E) provides that “[a]ll records, reports and other documentation made or received by a private postsecondary institution police department are public records and are subject to” the Arizona Public Records Law.

If GCU withholds or redacts any information, please provide the specific provision of the law being used to withhold or redact that information pursuant to A.R.S. § 39-121.01(D)(2).

Please provide the records “promptly,” as required by A.R.S. § 39-121.01. “‘Prompt,’ … mean[s] ‘quick to act or to do what is required,’ or ‘done, spoken, etc. at once or without delay.’” W. Valley View, Inc. v. Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office, 216 Ariz. 225, 230, ¶ 21, 165 P.3d 203, 208 (App. 2007) (quoting Webster’s New World Dictionary 1137 (2d ed. 1980)). See also Phoenix New Times, L.L.C. v. Arpaio, 217 Ariz. at 547, ¶ 49, 177 P.3d at 289 (App. 2008).

Invisible Institute is an award-winning nonprofit public accountability journalism organization based in Chicago. Our mission is to enhance the capacity of citizens to hold public institutions accountable. Because I, the requestor, am a journalist, and I seek the information in order to disseminate it to the public, and because the information regards the safety, welfare, and legal rights of the general public, I request a fee waiver for this data. It is not for commercial benefit because I am a member of the nonprofit news media whose principal purpose is to access and disseminate information regarding the general public’s safety, welfare, and legal rights. To the extent you intend to assess any charges, please notify me to discuss first.

Thank you for your time and prompt attention to this matter. Please contact me should you have any questions related to this request.

Sam Stecklow
Invisible Institute

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Sam Stecklow

communications.subject: RE: Arizona Public Records Law Request: GCUPD Policies
communications.communication:
Mr. Stecklow,

Grand Canyon University has received your request for public records. I have been designated your single point of contact and production will proceed on a rolling basis. Records research has begun and barring any unforeseen issues, my goal is to present all requested records within the next 30 business days.

You have identified your request as non-commercial. Arizona law provides remedies if public records are obtained or used for a commercial purpose without proper disclosure or used for a different commercial purpose. See A.R.S. 39-121.03(B)-(C).

Sincerely,

Sonia Michel, MBA

title: Flock Safety ALPR Records Request - Riverside County Sheriff's Office

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records Request - Riverside County Sheriff's Office
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting the following records related to Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems operated by or in partnership with the Riverside County Sheriff's Office:

1. CONTRACTS/AGREEMENTS: All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, amendments, renewals, or other legal instruments between the Riverside County Sheriff's Office and Flock Safety, Inc., including pricing, terms of service, and data governance provisions.

2. CAMERA INVENTORY & GPS LOCATIONS: A complete list of all Flock Safety ALPR cameras deployed within your jurisdiction, including each camera's physical address and/or GPS coordinates, installation date, and operational status.

3. SEARCH AUDIT LOGS: All audit logs, access logs, or query records showing who accessed the Flock Safety system, when, what license plates were queried, and the stated reason or case number associated with each query.

4. NETWORK/DATA SHARING RECORDS: All records describing which agencies, entities, or networks the Riverside County Sheriff's Office shares Flock Safety data with, including any Hot List sharing agreements, data sharing policies, and agency network participation records.

5. DETECTION/HIT STATISTICS: Aggregate statistics on the number of license plate reads, alerts, hits, or matches generated by Flock Safety ALPR cameras, broken down by time period if available.

6. CAMERA FOOTAGE/READS: All license plate read images, associated metadata (timestamp, location, plate number), and any video or image captures associated with the following individual and vehicle:
   - Name: Anthony Waldheim
   - California License Plate: 9HEE841
   Please search all available records within the system's retention period.

I request records in electronic format. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record or portion thereof.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anthony Waldheim

Once your email address has been verified, you can log in to the system to update any contact or password information and to track the progress of your request.

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: C001220-050326
communications.communication:
Dear Anthony Waldheim: 
 
Thank you for submitting a request for public records to Riverside County Sheriff's Department. Your request was received in this office on 5/3/2026 and given the reference number C001220-050326 for tracking purposes. 
 Records Requested: " To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting the following records related to Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems operated by or in partnership with the Riverside County Sheriff's Office: 

1. CONTRACTS/AGREEMENTS: All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, amendments, renewals, or other legal instruments between the Riverside County Sheriff's Office and Flock Safety, Inc., including pricing, terms of service, and data governance provisions. 

2. CAMERA INVENTORY & GPS LOCATIONS: A complete list of all Flock Safety ALPR cameras deployed within your jurisdiction, including each camera's physical address and/or GPS coordinates, installation date, and operational status. 

3. SEARCH AUDIT LOGS: All audit logs, access logs, or query records showing who accessed the Flock Safety system, when, what license plates were queried, and the stated reason or case number associated with each query. 

4. NETWORK/DATA SHARING RECORDS: All records describing which agencies, entities, or networks the Riverside County Sheriff's Office shares Flock Safety data with, including any Hot List sharing agreements, data sharing policies, and agency network participation records. 

5. DETECTION/HIT STATISTICS: Aggregate statistics on the number of license plate reads, alerts, hits, or matches generated by Flock Safety ALPR cameras, broken down by time period if available. 

6. CAMERA FOOTAGE/READS: All license plate read images, associated metadata (timestamp, location, plate number), and any video or image captures associated with the following individual and vehicle: 
- Name: Anthony Waldheim 
- California License Plate: 9HEE841 
Please search all available records within the system's retention period. 

I request records in electronic format. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record or portion thereof. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Anthony Waldheim 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2279421/" 
Pursuant to California Government Code § 7922.535, staff will review your request and respond within 10 days to notify you if there are responsive records, or if an extension is necessary to determine if responsive records exist.  If your request was received after business hours or on a weekend or holiday, the next business day will be considered the date of receipt. The 10-day response period starts with the first calendar day after the date of receipt. (Civ. Code § 10.)  You may be contacted by the department if there are questions regarding your request. 
 
PLEASE NOTE: The California Public Records Act (CPRA) does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Records Request System. 
Riverside County Sheriff's Department

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting the following records related to Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems operated by or in partnership with the Riverside County Sheriff's Office: 

1. CONTRACTS/AGREEMENTS: All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, amendments, renewals, or other legal instruments between the Riverside County Sheriff's Office and Flock Safety, Inc., including pricing, terms of service, and data governance provisions. 

2. CAMERA INVENTORY & GPS LOCATIONS: A complete list of all Flock Safety ALPR cameras deployed within your jurisdiction, including each camera's physical address and/or GPS coordinates, installation date, and operational status. 

3. SEARCH AUDIT LOGS: All audit logs, access logs, or query records showing who accessed the Flock Safety system, when, what license plates were queried, and the stated reason or case number associated with each query. 

4. NETWORK/DATA SHARING RECORDS: All records describing which agencies, entities, or networks the Riverside County Sheriff's Office shares Flock Safety data with, including any Hot List sharing agreements, data sharing policies, and agency network participation records. 

5. DETECTION/HIT STATISTICS: Aggregate statistics on the number of license plate reads, alerts, hits, or matches generated by Flock Safety ALPR cameras, broken down by time period if available. 

6. CAMERA FOOTAGE/READS: All license plate read images, associated metadata (timestamp, location, plate number), and any video or image captures associated with the following individual and vehicle: 
- Name: Anthony Waldheim 
- California License Plate: 9HEE841 
Please search all available records within the system's retention period. 

I request records in electronic format. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption(s) relied upon for each withheld record or portion thereof. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Anthony Waldheim 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2279421/" ***Specific to Item 1, 3, and 4*** 
The Riverside County Sheriff’s Office has located the records responsive to your request, and those records are available in the Records Request System. Please log in to your online account to retrieve the records. 
While the responsive records were disclosed, the Office has redacted information for privacy reasons. Where non-disclosable portions of public records are “reasonably segregable” from the disclosable portions, the agency may withhold or redact the non-disclosable portions while producing the disclosable portions. Thus, redactions are permitted, as long as the basis for the redaction (specified exemption) is identified in the agency’s response. See Cal. Gov. Code § 7922.525(a)-(b) American Civ. Liberties Union Found’n v. Deukmejian (1982) 32 Cal.3d 440, 452-454. 

The Office has asserted the below exemptions as legal authority to redact portions of our disclosed records: 

Government Code § 7922.000 - the records in question are exempt because, under the facts of this particular case, the public interest served by not disclosing the record clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure of the record. 

Government Code § 7927.705, read in conjunction with Article 1, § 1 of the California Constitution provides that personal information details are subject to privacy rights that make such information exempt from disclosure. 
***Specific to Item 5*** 
The Riverside County Sheriff’s Office has located the records responsive to your request, and those records are available in the Records Request System. Please log in to your online account to retrieve the records. ***Specific to Items 2 and 6*** The Riverside County Sheriff's Office has reviewed your request and has determined that the records requested are exempt from disclosure under the California Public Records Act (CPRA) for the following reasons: 
Government Code § 7922.000 – the records in question are exempt because under the facts of the particular case the public interest served by not disclosing the record clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure of the record. 

Government Code § 7923.600(a) – provides that law enforcement investigation, security and intelligence information file records of a local police agency are exempt from disclosure. Furthermore, as set forth in Rivero v. Superior Court, 54 Cal. App. 4th 1048 (1997) and Williams v. Superior Court, 5 Cal, 4th 337 (1993), the investigatory file exemption to the public records act request is not lost even where the investigation is deemed concluded or terminated. 

Government Code § 7927.705 – read in conjunction with Evidence Code § 1040, provides that official information acquired in confidence by a public employee is exempt from disclosure. 

Government Code § 7927.705 – read in conjunction with Article 1, § 1 of the California Constitution provides that personal information details are subject to privacy rights that make such information exempt from disclosure. 
This concludes your CPRA request.. 
Sincerely, 

Lieutenant Francisco Velasco 
CPRA Unit 
Riverside County Sheriff's Office

title: Arrest on Evans Ave Near RTC 4th Street Station 9pm-9:21 pm April 4, 2026

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Arrest on Evans Ave Near RTC 4th Street Station 9pm-9:21 pm April 4, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

2 vehicles with City of Reno Police Department were seen arresting an driver April 4, 2026 from 9:00 pm to 9:21 p.m on Evans Ave near RTC 4th Street Station, and the driver was seen being put into handcuffs and placed into back of the patrol vehicle.

I'm requesting information on what the male suspect's age & name is, the reason for arrest, what are all charges the driver is facing, what was the driver's blood level result and how much higher was his level over the legal limit since D.U.I was involved? What drug did the driver test positive for that resulted in D.U.I or was it alcohol drinking?

How much time will the driver spend behind bars and what facility was the arrested driver taken to for sentencing to be served?

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

ROBERT RINCON

communications.subject: General Public Records :: G020073-040726
communications.communication:
Dear Ricky : 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of Reno. 
Your request was received in this office on April 07, 2026 and given the reference number: #G020073-040726 for tracking purposes.  Request Details: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All records from January 1st, 2023 to December 31st, 2024 regarding settlements for claims or lawsuits alleging police misconduct by the City of Reno Police Department. Please be sure to include settlement agreements, complaint summaries, amounts paid, and any related investigative findings. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Ricky Padilla 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259941/ 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate City department(s) to locate the information requested. Pursuant to NRS 239.0107 we will notify you within five (5) business days of receipt of the anticipated date your records will be available, and any estimated cost to produce them. Please note any confidential, proprietary, or protected information will be redacted prior to the release of your records. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Again, thank you for using the City of Reno Public Records Center.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Clay County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Clay County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: John Sparkman

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: John Sparkman
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation into John Jackson Sparkman (December 20, 1899 – November 16, 1985), who represented Alabama in the Senate from 1946 to 1979. Proof of death is not required as his date of birth was over 100 years ago.

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante
