communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Records in response to your public disclosure request C249698-121525 received on December 15, 2025 are now available. The cost for the records is as follows: 
 
Invoice Number: INV26-C249698-2 
Total: $1.25 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system.

  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login.

  * Select 'Done'.

Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request.

communications.subject: Economic Development :: C249698-121525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Dear Rose Terse, 
Records are now available in response to your request C249698-121525 regarding the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Section 8.17.7(iii) of the City's World Cup Host City Agreement requires that "no other substantial cultural events [...] other than concerts or Host City Event approved by FIFA [...] shall be organised or staged in the Host City within a period commencing one (1) day prior to a Match Day or opening ceremony taking place at a day prior to the Opening Match and ending one (1) day after a match day." 

Please provide all communications with FIFA, the Local Organizing Committee, and other parties representing FIFA including but not limited to emails, chat messages (including Microsoft Teams, Slack, Jabber, Zoom, WhatsApp, Signal), text messages, and voice mails regarding events seeking and/or receiving/being denied such approval. Please include any attachments and associated metadata. For chat messages, please provide all messages in the chat within 1 hour for context. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2152234/ 
The records being made available to you at this time include the following: meeting agendas noting an event moratorium. 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: C249698-121525. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. 
Due to the size and scope of your request, we are providing records to you in installments. We anticipate that the next installment of records will be available on or about May 1. 
Sincerely, 
Office of Economic Development

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #C249698-121525
communications.communication:
Hello Kristin,

The Washington Court of Appeals Div I has explained that agencies providing records in installments "must make them with reasonable diligence." C.S.A., 32 Wn. App. 2d at 558

I have once again received only 2 documents with this installment. Would you please provide the strategy being employed for your search? It seems like the logical strategy would be to identify events in the targeted period and review communications regarding the approval process.

With any reasonable search strategy, it seems improbable to have received a total of 21 pages of responsive records, even accounting for any third party notice.

I am once again asking that you provide the full breadth of records I have requested without further delay.

Thank you,
Rose

communications.subject: Economic Development :: C249698-121525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Hi Rose, I understand your frustration. The thought process for how the search was conducted was that since we don’t have one place that has denied or permitted events moved for any particular reason, I did a pretty broad keyword search. This brought back thousands of documents that I am sorting through to find any records that relate to event organizers seeking approval or being denied a permit due to the timing of the FIFA World Cup. On top of this, responding to public records requests for the Office of Economic Development is not the only work I’m responsible for, nor is this my only request. I am working diligently to sort through those records and find any responsive records in a timely manner while balancing my other work. If you would like, I can focus the search on what was permitted. Or if there are specific events you are looking for, I can conduct a more focused search. I appreciate your patience as I continue to sort through the records. Cheers, Kristin

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #C249698-121525
communications.communication:
Hello Kristin,

Appreciate the explanation. Please limit the search to emails, contracts, and other agreements between the city and FIFA regarding cultural events in the time range defined in the Host City Agreement, prioritizing records regarding Trans Pride Seattle scheduled for June 26, 2026 and PrideFest Capitol Hill scheduled for June 27, 2026.

Thank you,
Rose

title: Request to ATF for all 2025 film and TV collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to ATF for all 2025 film and TV collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films and television productions that the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) has supported, in any capacity whatsoever, during the year 2025. Such a list, full or partial, maybe include, but should not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports or similar reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits (Houston Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits (Houston Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Flock Shared Networks Information from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
This should include: "Organization Name", "Networks Shared With Me", "Networks I'm Sharing" from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Concerned

communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: P138879-022526
communications.communication:
Dear Concerned Concerned: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Houston Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act. Your request was received in this office on 2/25/2026 and given the reference number P138879-022526 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Flock Shared Networks Information from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
This should include: "Organization Name", "Networks Shared With Me", "Networks I'm Sharing" from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data 
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data 
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information 
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Concerned 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2226525/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant Houston Police Department department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Houston Public Information Request Center. 
Houston Police Department

title: Efforts to remove books from Naval Academy library (Department of Defense, Office of the Secretary of Defense)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Efforts to remove books from Naval Academy library (Department of Defense, Office of the Secretary of Defense)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any document concerning the disposition, destruction, displacement, or removal of books from the Naval Academy library.

2. Any document reflecting the search terms utilized to remove books from the Naval Academy library.

3. Any communications to or from the Office of the Secretary of Defense concerning books at the Naval Academy library. 

4. Any communications to or from Vice Adm. Yvette Davids which mentions any of the following terms: library, book, books, DEI, diversity, EO, executive order, gender, ideology. This request does not include copies of news reports.

5. Any communications to or from Cmdr. Tim Hawkins which mentions any of the following terms: library, book, books, DEI, diversity, EO, executive order, gender, ideology. This request does not include copies of news reports.

These requests are limited to records generated on or after January 20, 2025.

"Communications" means and includes emails, text messages, social media messages, letters, memoranda, calendar entries, chats, instant messages, or any other form of one-to-one or one-to-many communication. "Communications" also includes transcripts, videos, audio, minutes, notes, or other recordings of conversations, meetings, phone calls, or Zoom (or similar) meetings.

Please note that this request seeks public records wherever they may be found, including on devices or networks owned or operated by private parties. For example, if an employee uses a personal cell phone or social media account to conduct public business, records created, maintained, or held on the phone or account are public records subject to this request.

REQUEST FOR EXPEDITED PROCESSING:

I request that this request be expedited under 32 CFR § 286.8(e)(1)(i)(B) for the following reasons:

1. I certify the following to be true and correct: My organization is primarily engaged in disseminating information. The Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE) maintains a news website at https://thefire.org/news, through which its staff members regularly publish news stories and editorial content concerning the activities of governments, government officials, and other persons or organizations of public concern. I am a staff member of FIRE. I frequently write about matters involving the First Amendment and press freedom, including on FIRE's website. See, e.g., https://www.thefire.org/news-archive?author=303. A substantial component of my duties as a staff member of FIRE is to obtain information through public records requests, including through the Freedom of Information Act, which I or my colleagues then share through FIRE's website and social media channels. I also publish news and other information of public concern via "X" (https://x.com/adamsteinbaugh) and "Bluesky" (https://bsky.app/profile/adamsteinbaugh.bsky.social).

2. The requested information concerns an actual or alleged government activity, namely the removal of books from a library due to their viewpoints or content.

3. The requested information extends beyond the public's right to know about government activity generally and instead has a particular value that would be lost if not processed on an expedited basis. The information pertains to a breaking news story of public interest. That issue has attracted significant media coverage. The information has value that would be lost if not processed on an expedited basis.

Media coverage evidencing the significant public interest in this matter includes:
https://www.nytimes.com/2025/04/04/us/politics/naval-academy-dei-books-removed.html
https://www.washingtonpost.com/education/2025/04/02/naval-academy-remove-400-dei-books/
https://www.foxnews.com/politics/naval-academy-tosses-400-books-from-library-following-trump-dei-expulsion-orders
https://apnews.com/article/military-academies-dei-hegseth-trump-ba9731f24b4eb4bd9c02b568209f97af
https://blavity.com/jackie-robinson-mlk-books-naval-academy-dei-ban
https://foxbaltimore.com/news/local/naval-academy-will-not-remove-dei-related-materials-from-library
https://dailycaller.com/2025/04/02/naval-academy-removes-hundreds-of-books-that-push-dei/
https://www.washingtonexaminer.com/policy/defense/3367284/naval-academy-removes-400-books-dei-directive-hegseth/
https://www.baltimoresun.com/2025/04/02/hegseth-addresses-naval-academy-ahead-of-reported-book-ban/
https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/national-international/maya-angelou-memoir-holocaust-book-among-dei-purge-at-naval-academy-library/3671190/
https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/apr/03/naval-academy-dei-books
https://thehill.com/homenews/race-politics/5227843-naval-academy-library-books-dei-trump-hegseth/
https://thehill.com/opinion/national-security/5228318-the-navy-cant-fight-for-freedom-while-banning-books/
https://www.thecollegefix.com/u-s-naval-academy-removes-400-books-that-promote-dei-from-library/
https://saraacarter.com/naval-academy-removes-400-books-following-dei-crackdown/
https://www.academia.org/naval-academy-purges-dei-books-from-library/

REQUEST FOR A FEE WAIVER OR REDUCED FEES:

I request, under 32 CFR § 286.12(l), a waiver or reduction of fees for the following reasons:

1. Disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because its disclosure would shed light on the operations or activities of the government. The requested information pertains specifically to the policies, procedures, and practices of senior Department officials concerning their interaction with the media, implicating the Speech and Press clauses of the First Amendment.

2. Disclosure of the requested information would be likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of those operations or activities. Disclosure of the requested information would meaningfully inform the public about the Department's activities and operations, as the information pertains to the policies and underlying information driving decisions by senior Department officials. Disclosing the policies and other information would demonstrate the bases for those decisions, which have attracted substantial public attention, as demonstrated by the articles and press statements cited above. That information is not in the public domain.

3. Disclosure of the requested information will contribute to the understanding of a reasonably broad audience of persons interested in the subject, as opposed to the individual understanding of the requester. The requestor is a licensed attorney who practices First Amendment law, including its application to members of the press. As such, the requestor will be able to effectively convey information to the public. Further, the requestor is a representative of the news media. The requestor and his employer, the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression, gather information of potential interest to a segment of the public, turns that information into distinct works (including through its news website, located at https://thefire.org/news), and distributes that information to the public. The information requested here relates to information that is about current events or that would be of current interest to the public. The requestor's past publication record (in addition to FIRE's articles available at https://thefire.org/news) is available at https://www.thefire.org/news-archive?author=303.

4. The requested information is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester. The requestor works for a nonprofit organization and the records are not sought for a commercial purpose.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

Ileana Garnand". Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. title: Community ALPR Audit 2 (McLean County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit 2 (McLean County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs.

2. Misuse and Discipline Records

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present.

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims.

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention.

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy.

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm)

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed.

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches.

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about:
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies)
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting.

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including:
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained
- Procedures for deleting or purging data
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell

RE: Your FOIA request W012216-042826 dated 4/29/2026 Your request is too broad as written; therefore, this department is currently unable to conduct a search for the requested records. Please reply to this message and clarify your request by specifying dates in which you are requesting search records. 
If we have not heard from you regarding this request for clarification within 5 business days, we will presume you no longer wish to receive the records and this request will be closed (but can be re-opened if you respond), as we are unable to process it with the information you provided within the request. You have the right to appeal a closed request (i.e., a denial) by a) submitting a written request for review within 60 days of the denial to the Public Access Counselor, Office of the Illinois Attorney General, Public Access Bureau, 500 S. 2nd Street, Springfield, IL 62701 (phone number 1-877-299-3642) or b) filing suit for injunctive or declaratory relief in the McLean County Circuit Court.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request #W012216-042826
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response dated April 29, 2026. I note that your letter states my request is “too broad as written” but does not specify which particular items you find overbroad, explain why compliance with any item would be unduly burdensome, or identify the specific burden (e.g., volume of records, review time) as required under 5 ILCS 140/3(g).

In the interest of resolving this promptly and in good faith, I offer the following clarifications and date ranges:

For items 1, 2, 3, 8, and 9 (which request search records, discipline logs, false-positive reports, data-sharing details, and audit logs) let us specify the request from the date of Flock Safety system installation through April 30, 2026.

For items 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10, these request current and recently superseded written guidance documents. Please provide all versions of responsive documents currently in effect or that have been superseded since the Flock system’s installation.

If you would identify which item(s) you contend are burdensome, explain the specific burden, and propose a narrowing, we may confer further as the statute requires.

I look forward to your specific response so we may proceed. Per 5 ILCS 140/3(b), I anticipate a response within 5 business days.

RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PUBLIC RECORDS 
To: Brad Bell 
Email: 209557-07507955@requests.muckrock.com 
Address: MuckRock News, DEPT MR209557 263 Huntington Ave Boston MA 02115 
From FOIA Officer: Katlin Crow 
Department: COUNTY SHERIFF 
 
Response Date: 05/12/2026 RE: Your Sheriff FOIA request W012216-042826 dated 4/29/2026 
For the following records: 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. Ideological/Identity Based Search Records 

All Flock search records (including Organization Audit logs with purpose fields) where the stated reason, tag, or description references any protest, demonstration, march, rally, or political/social justice event or organization or refers to a protected characteristic such as race, gender, nationality, religion, disability, etc. including slang and unredacted slurs. 

2. Misuse and Discipline Records 

All internal affairs complaints, internal notes, investigative reports, disciplinary records, or personnel actions related to potential misuse of Flock Safety systems (including stalking, personal use, or unauthorized access) from installation to present. 

3. False Positives and Wrongful Stops 

All records documenting false-positive identifications, misidentifications, erroneous “hot list” alerts, or resulting wrongful stops/detentions tied to Flock data, including internal complaints, incident reports, or legal claims. 

4. Flock Raven/Audio Detection Deployment 

All records related to any Flock Safety Raven acoustic sensors or audio detection capabilities operated by this agency, including purchase orders, installation records, location lists, and any policies describing audio capture, storage, access, and retention. 

5. Audio Recording Legal Analysis 

All internal or external legal opinions, memoranda, or analyses regarding whether Flock Raven or similar audio-capable devices comply with Illinois eavesdropping law (720 ILCS 5/14-2), including assessments of potential recording inside vehicles, homes, or locations with a reasonable expectation of privacy. 

6. AI Features Usage (FreeForm) 

All records describing or referencing the use of Flock’s FreeForm search feature or any AI-assisted search tool by this agency, including training materials, usage policies, internal audits, or guidance on when/how such features may be employed. 

7. Warrant vs. Warrantless Search Policy 

All written policies, procedures, or guidance addressing whether officers must obtain a warrant or court order before accessing Flock data or conducting Flock searches, including any limitations on historical data searches. 

8. External Agency Data Sharing Details 

Beyond the basic authorized agencies list, please provide any available details about: 
- Specific types of data shared with each external agency 
- Frequency or volume of sharing with major recipients (particularly federal agencies) 
- Any memoranda of understanding or data-sharing agreements governing external access 

9. Internal Search Purpose Transparency 

The Flock Organization Audit report showing all internal searches with the full, unredacted “purpose” or “reason” field for each search, in machine-readable format (CSV/spreadsheet), to assess patterns of use and potential discriminatory targeting. 

10. Data Retention and Deletion Policies 

All records describing this agency’s data retention and deletion practices for Flock Safety data, including: 
- Written policies on how long Flock data is retained 
- Procedures for deleting or purging data 
- Any records of incidents where data was retained beyond stated periods or not properly deleted 
- Communications with Flock Safety regarding data retention capabilities or limitations 
- Audits or a lack thereof which verify data is actually deleted and shared only as per stated policy 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Brad Bell 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2274002/

Records in the possession of any public body created in the course of administrative enforcement proceedings, and any law enforcement or correctional agency for law enforcement purposes, but only to the extent that disclosure would disclose unique or specialized investigative techniques other than those generally used and known or disclose internal documents of correctional agencies related to detection, observation or investigation of incidents of crime or misconduct, and disclosure would result in demonstrable harm to the agency or public body that is the recipient of the request;

title: RFP #1231 – Williamson County Schools Outsourced Lawncare Services

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: RFP #1231 – Williamson County Schools Outsourced Lawncare Services
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

At your earliest convenience, I am requesting the following items that resulted from RFP #1231 – Williamson County Schools Outsourced Lawncare Services:
- Final bid tabulations 
- All Executed agreements with awarded vendors (D&D Lawncare, Greenway, Holton Landscapes, and Southbound Erosions) for base and option years
- All names of previous members of the selection committee and associated deliberation notes on award decision

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Drek Hawkins.

Best,

title: ALPR audit

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: ALPR audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Concerning Flock Safety, these files;

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Typically the output for these audits is available in monthly batches.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Traci
